attacking_style: Explosive attacks in final 3-10km of climbs, particularly targeting gradient changes and steep finales. Willing to go long (20km+) when exploiting terrain knowledge, but late-race accelerations (sub-5km) most common.
team_tactics: Minimal domestique support from Trek-Segafredo, often attacking solo or supporting teammates rather than receiving organized lead-outs. Frequently isolated early on climbs, forcing self-reliant racing.
terrain_preference: Steep gradient changes where explosive power matters over sustained watts. Struggles on moderate gradients against diesel engines like Froome. Excels on technical descents (caught leader final 2km downhill). Exposed in crosswinds and flat echelon situations.
vulnerability: Severe weakness in crosswinds/echelon positioning (caught multiple times). Struggles on moderate-gradient sustained climbs against high-tempo trains. Steep finales (10%+) revealed major weakness late-career. Poor TTT support chronically handicapped GC.
key_patterns: Attacks predominantly final 3-10km of climbs, rarely sustaining long-range efforts successfully (7 instances)
Cannot match sustained Sky/tempo pace on moderate gradients, needs explosive terrain (4 observations)
Crosswind vulnerability severe: caught behind echelons requiring team rescues repeatedly (3 instances)
Third-week form exceptional even in decline, career-best performances final Vuelta week (3 observations)
Weak TTT/team support forces solo tactics, Trek 1:15+ down vs top squads (2 instances)
Legacy motivation produces outlier performances: attacked career finale, jumped 9th to 5th overall
form_trajectory: negative: Despite third-week Vuelta heroics, overall 2017-2018 showed declining power on steep gradients, crash-compromised 2018 Tour, and chronic positioning vulnerabilities. Legacy-driven peaks masked structural decline.
